context: Tests whether the agent uses the correct discovery npm commands ('npm run discover-tripit' and 'npm run discover-reclaim') with the exact project directory path for each service's diagnostic script.

checklist.name: TripIt discover command
checklist.description: debug-tripit.sh uses 'npm run discover-tripit' — not 'npm run sync', 'npm run debug', or any other command

checklist.name: TripIt cd before command
checklist.description: debug-tripit.sh navigates (cd) into the project directory before or as part of running the npm command

checklist.name: Reclaim discover command
checklist.description: debug-reclaim.sh uses 'npm run discover-reclaim' — not 'npm run sync', 'npm run debug', or any other command

checklist.name: Reclaim cd before command
checklist.description: debug-reclaim.sh navigates (cd) into the project directory before or as part of running the npm command

checklist.name: Page structure purpose
checklist.description: debug-runbook.md explains that these scripts dump the page/DOM structure seen by the automation tool (not just 'run diagnostics')

checklist.name: When to use each
checklist.description: debug-runbook.md distinguishes when to use the TripIt diagnostic vs the Reclaim diagnostic (e.g., based on which side of the sync is failing)

checklist.name: Screenshots directory output
checklist.description: At least one output file mentions that discovery dumps artifacts (screenshots, HTML, DOM outline) into the 'screenshots/' directory
